title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. kerneri
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. kerneri, also known as Bugleweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in reducing symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative effects. Bugleweed is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, which may also help lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Herbal preparations of Bugleweed are often used in naturopathic and homeopathic medicine to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
